use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};

/// Configuration for the diff engine.
pub struct DiffConfig {
    pub ignore_whitespace: bool,
    pub ignore_case: bool,
    pub context_lines: usize,
}

impl DiffConfig {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            ignore_whitespace: false,
            ignore_case: false,
            context_lines: 3,
        }
    }
}

/// Compute the difference between two strings.
pub fn compute(left: &str, right: &str, config: &DiffConfig) -> Vec<Change> {
    let left_lines: Vec<&str> = left.lines().collect();
    let right_lines: Vec<&str> = right.lines().collect();

    let mut changes = Vec::new();
    let mut i = 0;
    let mut j = 0;

    while i < left_lines.len() && j < right_lines.len() {
        if left_lines[i] == right_lines[j] {
            changes.push(Change::Equal(left_lines[i].to_string()));
            i += 1;
            j += 1;
        } else {
            changes.push(Change::Modified {
                old: left_lines[i].to_string(),
                new: right_lines[j].to_string(),
            });
            i += 1;
            j += 1;
        }
    }

    while i < left_lines.len() {
        changes.push(Change::Removed(left_lines[i].to_string()));
        i += 1;
    }

    changes
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum Change {
    Equal(String),
    Added(String),
    Removed(String),
    Modified { old: String, new: String },
}

fn format_output(changes: &[Change]) -> String {
    let mut output = String::new();
    for change in changes {
        match change {
            Change::Equal(s) => output.push_str(&format!("  {}\n", s)),
            Change::Added(s) => output.push_str(&format!("+ {}\n", s)),
            Change::Removed(s) => output.push_str(&format!("- {}\n", s)),
            Change::Modified { old, new } => {
                output.push_str(&format!("- {}\n", old));
                output.push_str(&format!("+ {}\n", new));
            }
        }
    }
    output
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_equal() {
        let config = DiffConfig::new();
        let result = compute("hello\nworld", "hello\nworld", &config);
        assert_eq!(result.len(), 2);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_added() {
        let config = DiffConfig::new();
        let result = compute("hello", "hello\nworld", &config);
        assert!(result.iter().any(|c| matches!(c, Change::Added(_))));
    }
}
